Our Adventures

From the iconic Mokulua Islands to the tranquil waters of Kaneohe Bay. Pick your adventure and let's get outside.

easy
3 hours

Kailua E-Bike Coastal Explorer

Kailua Town, Oahu

  • Cruise Kailua's scenic coastal bike paths on a premium e-bike
  • Visit Lanikai Beach — consistently ranked among the world's best
  • Stop at the Pali Lookout for breathtaking views
$99 per person
Book Now
moderate
Flexible (guided tours from 4–5 hours)

Kayak Kailua — The Complete Guide to Paddling Oahu's Windward Waters

Kailua Beach, Oahu

  • Paddle from Oahu's most beginner-friendly launch at Kailua Beach Park
  • Multiple route options: the Mokulua Islands, Flat Island, Popoia, or inside the reef
  • Protected waters inside Kailua Bay — calmer than North Shore, warmer than Hawaii Kai
$149 per person (guided tour)
Book Now
easy
4 hours

Kaneohe Bay Kayak & Sandbar

Kaneohe Bay, Oahu

  • Paddle to the famous Kaneohe Sandbar (Ahu o Laka)
  • Crystal-clear protected waters inside the bay
  • Views of the Ko'olau Mountains and Chinaman's Hat
$129 per person
Book Now
moderate
5 hours

Mokulua Islands Kayak Adventure

Kailua Beach, Oahu

  • Paddle to the iconic Mokulua Islands off Kailua Beach
  • Explore seabird sanctuary and tide pools
  • Snorkel with Hawaiian green sea turtles
$149 per person
Book Now

Why Adventure With Us

We're Oahu locals who know these waters, trails, and coastlines better than anyone. Every tour is led by passionate guides who put safety, fun, and authentic Hawaiian experiences first.

Safety First

All guides are CPR/First Aid certified. Top-quality gear, thorough briefings, and weather monitoring on every tour.

Local Experts

Born-and-raised Oahu guides who share insider knowledge, history, and hidden gems you won't find in guidebooks.

No Experience Needed

Our tours are designed for all skill levels. From beginners to experienced adventurers, we make sure everyone has a great time.

Free Cancellation

Plans change! Cancel up to 24 hours before your tour for a full refund. Weather-related cancellations are always free.

Ready for Your Oahu Adventure?

Reserve your spot today. Tours fill up fast — especially during peak season. Use our live booking calendar below to check availability and secure your date.

Book on FareHarbor 📞 (808) 555-1234

Free cancellation up to 24 hours before your tour. Weather-related cancellations always free.

Got Questions? We've Got Answers

The tour covers approximately 12 miles at a relaxed pace with multiple stops. The e-bike makes the distance effortless — even on hills!
Yes, basic bike riding ability is required. If you haven't ridden in a while, we offer a quick refresher before departure.
Riders must be at least 14 years old and 5'0" tall. Riders under 18 must be accompanied by an adult.
Kailua Bay is uniquely suited for kayaking because of its protected reef, consistent but manageable trade winds, and iconic island destinations like the Mokuluas. Unlike the North Shore (which can have massive winter surf) or Hawaii Kai (which lacks the same open-ocean island destinations), Kailua offers the best balance of adventure and accessibility year-round. The bay's geography creates a natural paddling corridor that's exciting but rarely dangerous, and the view of the Ko'olau Mountains from the water is unmatched anywhere on the island.
No — that's one of the best things about Kailua. Complete beginners can paddle here safely with a guide. The bay is protected by a large barrier reef that keeps swells small, and the water is warm and shallow near shore. Our guides provide thorough instruction before launch, and 90% of first-timers feel confident within the first 15 minutes on the water. That said, if you have never kayaked and plan to go without a guide, we strongly recommend sticking inside the reef and avoiding offshore winds.
There are four main routes: (1) The Mokulua Islands — our signature 5-hour guided tour to the twin islets with snorkeling and tide pool exploration; (2) Flat Island — a shorter, easier paddle to a small island just offshore, great for families; (3) Popoia Island — a less-visited bird sanctuary island with good snorkeling; (4) Inside the Reef — a relaxed route staying inside the protective barrier reef, ideal for beginners and photographers. Your guide will choose the best route based on conditions that day.

🌊 Active Oahu Tours

Guided kayak, e-bike, and outdoor adventures on Oahu's stunning windward coast. Experience the real Hawaii with local guides who call these waters home.
